271	
272	    ``dimension`` is a stable string in the same open family the gate schema
273	    uses (e.g. ``source.types``, ``source.language``, ``date.recency``,
274	    ``content.coverage``, ``content.comparison``, ``deliverable.format``,
275	    ``deliverable.length``, ``rhetoric.tone``, ``source.named``,
276	    ``source.scope_facet``, ``source.jurisdiction``).
277	    """
278	
279	    dimension: str
280	    value: Optional[str]
281	    force: str = FORCE_PREFER          # observed force; never invented as hard
282	    origin: str = ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC
283	    spans: list[PromptSpan] = field(default_factory=list)
284	    # free-form provenance so the reconciliation stays auditable (which raw
285	    # source(s) contributed, the raw trigger phrase, any op/strictness, etc.).
286	    detail: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
287	    # generic-IR annotations (registry-derived; OPTIONAL). ``normalization_status``
288	    # is 'opaque' for a kind the registry couldn't map — preserved, never dropped.
289	    subject: str = ""
290	    attribute: str = ""
291	    operator: str = ""
292	    stage_owner: str = ""
293	    normalization_status: str = ""     # exact | proposed | opaque
294	
295	    def canonical_dimension(self) -> str:
296	        row = _registry_row(self.dimension)
297	        return row.canonical if row else self.dimension
298	
299	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
300	        out = {
301	            "dimension": self.dimension,
302	            "value": self.value,
303	            "force": self.force,
304	            "origin": self.origin,
305	            "spans": [s.to_dict() for s in self.spans],
306	            "detail": dict(self.detail),
307	        }
308	        if self.subject:
309	            out["subject"] = self.subject
310	        if self.attribute:
311	            out["attribute"] = self.attribute
312	        if self.operator:
313	            out["operator"] = self.operator
314	        if self.stage_owner:
315	            out["stage_owner"] = self.stage_owner
316	        if self.normalization_status:
317	            out["normalization_status"] = self.normalization_status
318	        return out
319	
320	
321	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
322	# Span recovery (exact, quote-equality-guaranteed, never fabricated)
323	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
324	
325	def _locate_span(prompt: str, phrase: Optional[str]) -> Optional[PromptSpan]:
326	    """Return the first exact occurrence of ``phrase`` in ``prompt`` as a span.
327	
328	    Guarantees ``span.quote == prompt[span.start:span.end]``. Returns ``None``
329	    (no span) when ``phrase`` is empty or cannot be located verbatim — the
330	    adapter never invents an offset for a phrase it can't find.
331	    """
332	    if not prompt or not phrase:
333	        return None
334	    idx = prompt.find(phrase)
335	    if idx == -1:
336	        # Fall back to a case-insensitive locate, but keep the ACTUAL prompt
337	        # substring as the quote so quote-equality still holds exactly.
338	        low_i = prompt.lower().find(phrase.lower())
339	        if low_i == -1:
340	            return None
341	        return PromptSpan(low_i, low_i + len(phrase), prompt[low_i:low_i + len(phrase)])
342	    return PromptSpan(idx, idx + len(phrase), prompt[idx:idx + len(phrase)])
343	
344	
345	def _spans_for(prompt: str, phrases: list[Optional[str]]) -> list[PromptSpan]:
346	    """Locate each phrase; drop those not found; dedup by (start, end)."""
347	    out: list[PromptSpan] = []
348	    seen: set[tuple[int, int]] = set()
349	    for ph in phrases:
350	        sp = _locate_span(prompt, ph)
351	        if sp is None:
352	            continue
353	        key = (sp.start, sp.end)
354	        if key in seen:
355	            continue
356	        seen.add(key)
357	        out.append(sp)
358	    return out
359	
360	
361	def _stamp_ir(c: CandidateConstraint, *, opaque: bool = False) -> CandidateConstraint:
362	    """Fill a candidate's generic-IR fields from the registry (in place).
363	
364	    The candidate's RAW dimension keys the registry; the row supplies the
365	    canonical subject/attribute/operator/stage_owner. ``normalization_status`` is
366	    ``exact`` when the registry recognized the dimension, else ``opaque`` — an
367	    opaque candidate is a FIRST-CLASS constraint we preserve verbatim, never drop
368	    (the monotonic-lossless core principle). Returns the same candidate.
369	    """
370	    row = _registry_row(c.dimension)
371	    if row is not None:
372	        c.subject = row.subject
373	        c.attribute = row.attribute
374	        # exclusion flips IN→NOT_IN already encoded in the row; a hard "only X"
375	        # keeps the row operator. A soft candidate stays PREFER-shaped only when
376	        # the row itself is PREFER; otherwise the operator reflects the relation
377	        # (force carries hard/soft separately).
378	        c.operator = row.operator
379	        c.stage_owner = row.stage_owner
380	        c.normalization_status = "opaque" if opaque else "exact"
381	    else:
382	        # unknown dimension: keep it, mark opaque, route generically.
383	        c.subject = "source"
384	        c.attribute = c.dimension.split(".", 1)[-1] or "value"
385	        c.normalization_status = "opaque"
386	    return c
387	
388	
389	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
390	# Per-dimension candidate builders from each deterministic source
391	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
392	
393	def _from_user_constraints(
394	    prompt: str, uc: UserConstraints
395	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
396	    """Intake ``extract_constraints_regex`` -> candidates (dates, language,
397	    journal-only, timeline strictness)."""
398	    out: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
399	
400	    # Language (ISO code). Recover the span from the raw directive that named it.
401	    if uc.language:
402	        spans = _spans_for(prompt, list(uc.raw_directives))
403	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
404	            dimension="source.language",
405	            value=uc.language,
406	            force=FORCE_PREFER,
407	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
408	            spans=spans,
409	            detail={"source": "intake_regex", "raw_directives": list(uc.raw_directives)},
410	        )))
411	
412	    # Date window (start/end). Force reflects the extractor's own strictness.
413	    if uc.date_start_year is not None or uc.date_end_year is not None:
414	        force = FORCE_HARD if uc.timeline_strictness == "hard" else FORCE_PREFER
415	        trigger_phrases: list[Optional[str]] = list(uc.raw_directives)
416	        if uc.timeline_trigger_span:
417	            trigger_phrases.append(uc.timeline_trigger_span)
418	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
419	            dimension="date.recency",
420	            value=_date_value(uc),
421	            force=force,
422	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
423	            spans=_spans_for(prompt, trigger_phrases),
424	            detail={
425	                "source": "intake_regex",
426	                "date_start_iso": uc.date_start_iso(),
427	                "date_end_iso": uc.date_end_iso(),
428	                "timeline_strictness": uc.timeline_strictness,
429	                "timeline_trigger_span": uc.timeline_trigger_span,
430	            },
431	        )))
432	
433	    # Journal-only is EXTRACTED-but-DORMANT in the champion (operator veto). It
434	    # is a real deterministic signal, so it becomes a candidate — never a hard
435	    # gate from this path alone. SPAN-LOSS FIX (RECON-2 §2.2): the regex DID match
436	    # a verbatim substring; re-locate that exact match phrase in the prompt so the
437	    # candidate carries a real span and is promotable/explicit — not spans=[].
438	    if uc.journal_only:
439	        m = _JOURNAL_ONLY_RE.search(prompt or "")
440	        spans = _spans_for(prompt, [m.group(0)]) if m else []
441	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
442	            dimension="source.types",
443	            value="journal_article",
444	            force=FORCE_PREFER,
445	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
446	            spans=spans,
447	            detail={
448	                "source": "intake_regex",
449	                "journal_only_dormant": True,
450	                "raw_trigger": m.group(0) if m else "",
451	            },
452	        )))
453	
454	    return out
455	
456	
457	def _date_value(uc: UserConstraints) -> Optional[str]:
458	    lo = uc.date_start_iso()
459	    hi = uc.date_end_iso()
460	    if lo and hi:
461	        return f"{lo}..{hi}"
462	    return lo or hi
463	
464	
465	def _from_instruction_slots(
466	    prompt: str, slots: list[InstructionSlot]
467	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
468	    """Intake ``extract_instruction_slots_regex`` -> content/coverage candidates.
469	
470	    Comparisons, enumerations, requested topics and structure are coverage
471	    obligations (NOT headings — that mapping is deliberately not ported). Each
472	    slot's verbatim ``text`` recovers its span.
473	    """
474	    out: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
475	    for slot in slots:
476	        dim = {
477	            "comparison": "content.comparison",
478	            "enumeration": "content.coverage",
479	            "topic": "content.coverage",
480	            "structure": "deliverable.structure",
481	        }.get(slot.kind, "content.coverage")
482	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
483	            dimension=dim,
484	            value=", ".join(slot.entities) if slot.entities else slot.text,
485	            force=FORCE_PREFER,
486	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
487	            spans=_spans_for(prompt, [slot.text]),
488	            detail={
489	                "source": "intake_regex",
490	                "slot_id": slot.slot_id,
491	                "slot_kind": slot.kind,
492	                "entities": list(slot.entities),
493	            },
494	        )))
495	    return out
496	
497	
498	def _from_scope_constraints(
499	    prompt: str, sc: ScopeConstraints
500	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
501	    """Intake ``extract_scope_constraints_regex`` -> scope-facet / named-source
502	    candidates. Force mirrors the facet's own strictness (hard only where the
503	    extractor already saw an exclusivity/prohibition token)."""
504	    out: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
505	
506	    for facet in sc.facets:
507	        # GENERIC candidate→canonical (RECON-2 §4): route by the ONTOLOGY facet's
508	        # OWN ``dimension`` field, NOT by substring "journal"/"peer" matching. A
509	        # ``source_type`` facet → source.scope_facet (→ scope.source_types); a
510	        # ``language`` facet → source.language; jurisdiction/geography → their own
511	        # canonical dimension. Adding a facet family is data (a YAML block), not
512	        # a Python branch.
513	        fdim = (facet.dimension or "source_type").strip().lower()
514	        dim = {
515	            "jurisdiction": "source.jurisdiction",
516	            "geography": "source.jurisdiction",
517	            "language": "source.language",
518	        }.get(fdim, "source.scope_facet")
519	        # An ``exclude`` op is a NOT_IN exclusion, not an inclusion.
520	        if facet.op == "exclude":
521	            dim = "content.exclusion"
522	        force = FORCE_HARD if facet.strictness == "hard" else FORCE_PREFER
523	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
524	            dimension=dim,
525	            value=facet.facet_id,
526	            force=force,
527	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
528	            # locate the RAW trigger phrase the user wrote (facet.trigger_span is a
529	            # verbatim prompt slice), NOT the canonical facet_id — the span-loss-
530	            # safe pattern this whole rebuild generalizes (RECON-2 §2.4).
531	            spans=_spans_for(prompt, [facet.trigger_span]),
532	            detail={
533	                "source": "intake_regex",
534	                "op": facet.op,
535	                "strictness": facet.strictness,
536	                "facet_dimension": facet.dimension,
537	                "raw_trigger": facet.trigger_span,
538	            },
539	        )))
540	
541	    for named in list(sc.named_include) + list(sc.named_exclude):
542	        # A named-exclude is always hard (identity-enforced downstream); a
543	        # named-include is a boost (prefer).
544	        force = FORCE_HARD if named.strictness == "hard" else FORCE_PREFER
545	        dim = "source.named" if named.op != "exclude" else "content.exclusion"
546	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
547	            dimension=dim,
548	            value=named.label,
549	            force=force,
550	            origin=ORIGIN_DETERMINISTIC,
551	            spans=_spans_for(prompt, [named.label]),
552	            detail={
553	                "source": "intake_regex",
554	                "op": named.op,
555	                "strictness": named.strictness,
556	                "identity": dict(named.identity),
557	                "raw_trigger": named.label,
558	            },
559	        )))
560	
561	    return out
562	
563	
564	def _from_rule_reader(
565	    prompt: str, rr: Constraints
566	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
567	    """Ported adversarial rule-reader ``Constraints`` -> candidates.
568	
569	    The rule-reader carries no character offsets, so spans are recovered by
570	    locating each value verbatim in the prompt. When a value's phrase is not
571	    present verbatim (the LLM paraphrased it), the candidate is still emitted but
572	    with an empty span list — never a fabricated offset. Exclusions are the only
573	    rule-reader dimension that carries ``hard`` force (an explicit "exclude").
574	    """
575	    out: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
576	
577	    for st in rr.source_types:
578	        # SPAN-LOSS FIX (RECON-2 §2.1): the rule-reader emits a CANONICAL token
579	        # ("journal_article"); the prompt says "journal articles". Locating the
580	        # canonical token verbatim misses. Try the canonical token first, then its
581	        # de-canonicalized surface form (underscore→space) so a source-type the
582	        # user DID write recovers a real span (and can be promoted/explicit).
583	        # A quality marker ("high_quality") reroutes to source.quality (data map).
584	        dim, val = _reroute_quality("source.types", st)
585	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
586	            dimension=dim, value=val, force=FORCE_PREFER,
587	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER,
588	            spans=_spans_for(prompt, [st, str(st).replace("_", " ")]),
589	            detail={"source": "rule_reader", "raw_value": st},
590	        )))
591	    for lang in rr.languages:
592	        # The rule-reader emits a NORMALIZED ISO code (e.g. "en"); a bare 2-char
593	        # code matches stray substrings ("...intellig-en-ce..."), so we do NOT
594	        # try to span-locate it. The deterministic intake pass owns the real
595	        # language span; on overlap the merge keeps that authoritative span.
596	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
597	            dimension="source.language", value=lang, force=FORCE_PREFER,
598	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=[],
599	            detail={"source": "rule_reader", "no_span": "normalized_iso_code"},
600	        )))
601	    if rr.recency:
602	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
603	            dimension="date.recency", value=rr.recency, force=FORCE_PREFER,
604	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [rr.recency]),
605	            detail={"source": "rule_reader"},
606	        )))
607	    for cov in rr.required_coverage:
608	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
609	            dimension="content.coverage", value=cov, force=FORCE_PREFER,
610	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [cov]),
611	            detail={"source": "rule_reader"},
612	        )))
613	    for exc in rr.exclusions:
614	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
615	            dimension="content.exclusion", value=exc, force=FORCE_HARD,
616	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [exc]),
617	            detail={"source": "rule_reader", "kind": "exclusion"},
618	        )))
619	    if rr.format:
620	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
621	            dimension="deliverable.format", value=rr.format, force=FORCE_PREFER,
622	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [rr.format]),
623	            detail={"source": "rule_reader"},
624	        )))
625	    if rr.length:
626	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
627	            dimension="deliverable.length", value=rr.length, force=FORCE_PREFER,
628	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [rr.length]),
629	            detail={"source": "rule_reader"},
630	        )))
631	    if rr.tone:
632	        out.append(_stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
633	            dimension="rhetoric.tone", value=rr.tone, force=FORCE_PREFER,
634	            origin=ORIGIN_RULE_READER, spans=_spans_for(prompt, [rr.tone]),
635	            detail={"source": "rule_reader"},
636	        )))
637	
638	    return out
639	
640	
641	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
642	# Reconciliation (deterministic wins on overlap; rule-reader fills gaps)
643	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
644	
645	# Overlap is keyed by the CANONICAL (subject, attribute, value) — NOT the raw
646	# (dimension, value). This reconciles aliases expressed under different raw
647	# dimensions (rule-reader ``source.types=journal_article`` and intake
648	# ``source.scope_facet=peer_reviewed_journal`` both canonicalize to
649	# source/kind/journal…), which the old exact-(dimension,value) key never merged
650	# (RECON-2 §3). Values still normalize the same way across the two sources.
651	def _overlap_key(c: CandidateConstraint) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
652	    row = _registry_row(c.dimension)
653	    subj = row.subject if row else (c.subject or "source")
654	    attr = row.attribute if row else (c.attribute or c.dimension)
655	    return (subj, attr, (c.value or "").strip().casefold())
656	
657	
658	def _merge_pair(
659	    det: CandidateConstraint, rr: CandidateConstraint
660	) -> CandidateConstraint:
661	    """Deterministic candidate wins on span + force; record that both agreed and
662	    union the spans (the rule-reader may have located a span the regex path
663	    didn't, and vice-versa)."""
664	    merged_spans = list(det.spans)
665	    seen = {(s.start, s.end) for s in merged_spans}
666	    for s in rr.spans:
667	        if (s.start, s.end) not in seen:
668	            merged_spans.append(s)
669	            seen.add((s.start, s.end))
670	    detail = dict(det.detail)
671	    detail["also_seen_by"] = "rule_reader"
672	    detail["rule_reader_detail"] = dict(rr.detail)
673	    return _stamp_ir(CandidateConstraint(
674	        dimension=det.dimension,
675	        value=det.value,            # deterministic value is authoritative
676	        force=det.force,            # deterministic force is authoritative
677	        origin=ORIGIN_MERGED,
678	        spans=merged_spans,
679	        detail=detail,
680	    ))
681	
682	
683	def reconcile_candidates(
684	    prompt: str,
685	    *,
686	    rule_reader: "Constraints | dict[str, Any] | None" = None,
687	    ontology: "dict[str, Any] | None" = None,
688	) -> list[CandidateConstraint]:
689	    """Reconcile the deterministic intake extractors with the rule-reader.
690	
691	    Parameters
692	    ----------
693	    prompt:
694	        The raw task prompt (spans are recovered against this exact text).
695	    rule_reader:
696	        The ported adversarial rule-reader result — a
697	        :class:`~...instruction.constraint_extractor.Constraints`, its
698	        ``to_dict()`` form, or ``None`` to reconcile the intake extractors
699	        alone. This adapter NEVER runs the live rule-reader itself; the caller
700	        supplies an already-computed result (keeping the adapter offline/pure).
701	    ontology:
702	        Optional scope ontology override forwarded to
703	        ``extract_scope_constraints_regex`` (tests inject a fixture; production
704	        loads the default).
705	
706	    Returns
707	    -------
708	    list[CandidateConstraint]
709	        High-recall candidates with exact spans + origin. Deterministic
710	        candidates come first (they win on overlap); rule-reader-only candidates
711	        follow. This is a candidate list, NOT a pinned contract.
712	    """
713	    prompt = prompt or ""
714	
715	    # --- deterministic pass (the three champion intake regex extractors) ---
716	    uc = extract_constraints_regex(prompt)
717	    slots = extract_instruction_slots_regex(prompt)
718	    try:
719	        sc = extract_scope_constraints_regex(prompt, ontology=ontology)
720	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: no scope facets on ontology miss
721	        sc = ScopeConstraints(source="regex")
722	
723	    deterministic: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
724	    deterministic.extend(_from_user_constraints(prompt, uc))
725	    deterministic.extend(_from_instruction_slots(prompt, slots))
726	    deterministic.extend(_from_scope_constraints(prompt, sc))
727	
728	    # --- rule-reader pass (gap-filler) ---
729	    rr_obj: Optional[Constraints] = None
730	    if isinstance(rule_reader, Constraints):
731	        rr_obj = rule_reader
732	    elif isinstance(rule_reader, dict):
733	        rr_obj = _constraints_from_dict(rule_reader)
734	    rule_candidates = _from_rule_reader(prompt, rr_obj) if rr_obj is not None else []
735	
736	    # --- ONTOLOGY ALIAS NORMALIZATION (RECON-2 §3/§4): canonicalize every
737	    # source-type value to its ontology facet_id so a rule-reader alias
738	    # ("journal_article") and an intake facet ("peer_reviewed_journal") collapse
739	    # to ONE canonical value and MERGE (deterministic-hard wins). An unmapped kind
740	    # is left verbatim + marked opaque (first-class, never dropped).
741	    alias_index = _ontology_source_type_index(ontology)
742	    if alias_index:
743	        for cand in deterministic + rule_candidates:
744	            if cand.dimension in ("source.types", "source.scope_facet"):
745	                canon, mapped = _canonicalize_source_type(cand.value, alias_index)
746	                cand.value = canon
747	                cand.dimension = "source.types"
748	                if not mapped:
749	                    cand.normalization_status = "opaque"
750	
751	    # --- reconcile: deterministic wins on overlap; rule-reader fills gaps ---
752	    by_key: dict[tuple[str, str], CandidateConstraint] = {}
753	    ordered: list[CandidateConstraint] = []
754	    for c in deterministic:
755	        key = _overlap_key(c)
756	        by_key[key] = c
757	        ordered.append(c)
758	
759	    for rc in rule_candidates:
760	        key = _overlap_key(rc)
761	        existing = by_key.get(key)
762	        if existing is not None and existing.origin != ORIGIN_RULE_READER:
763	            # overlap -> merge into the deterministic winner in place
764	            idx = ordered.index(existing)
765	            merged = _merge_pair(existing, rc)
766	            ordered[idx] = merged
767	            by_key[key] = merged
768	        elif existing is None:
769	            by_key[key] = rc
770	            ordered.append(rc)
771	        # (a rule-reader/rule-reader dup collapses onto the first)
772	
773	    return ordered
774	
775	
776	def _constraints_from_dict(d: dict[str, Any]) -> Constraints:
777	    """Rebuild a :class:`Constraints` from its ``to_dict()`` form (tolerant)."""
778	    def _list(v: Any) -> list[str]:
779	        return [str(x) for x in v] if isinstance(v, (list, tuple)) else ([] if v in (None, "") else [str(v)])
780	
781	    def _opt(v: Any) -> Optional[str]:
782	        return None if v in (None, "") else str(v)
783	
784	    return Constraints(
785	        source_types=_list(d.get("source_types")),
786	        languages=_list(d.get("languages")),
787	        recency=_opt(d.get("recency")),
788	        required_coverage=_list(d.get("required_coverage")),
789	        exclusions=_list(d.get("exclusions")),
790	        format=_opt(d.get("format")),
791	        length=_opt(d.get("length")),
792	        tone=_opt(d.get("tone")),
793	    )
794

1	"""Research Planning Gate compiler (S1) — contract + plan, OFFLINE-testable.
2	
3	Turns a raw task prompt into a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact` via two
4	bounded structured LLM calls:
5	
6	  1. **contract compiler** — deterministic candidates (S0 adapter) + the raw
7	     prompt → a typed :class:`ResearchContract` (every clause dispositioned,
8	     every term tagged origin/force/spans);
9	  2. **plan compiler** — the *validated* contract → a
10	     :class:`ResearchExecutionPlan` (threads, mandatory query intents, outline
11	     seed, coverage matrix, budget).
12	
13	Both calls go through the repo LLM gateway (:class:`OpenRouterClient`) resolved
14	to the small policy model (``z-ai/glm-5.2``, the same arm FS query-gen uses).
15	The live call is OFF by default: the whole module is spend-free and hermetic
16	unless a flag is set OR a ``client=`` stub is injected (tests do the latter).
17	
18	Guardrail posture
19	-----------------
20	* **Default-OFF / None-default.** ``run_research_planning_gate`` requires an
21	  explicit ``client`` (a stub in tests) OR the live flag
22	  ``PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1``. With neither, and no client, it raises rather
23	  than silently hitting the network — the OFF path never fires an LLM.
24	* **Autonomous mode is pure ``contract→contract``.** It NEVER returns
25	  ``needs_input``, never blocks, never waits for a human: every would-ask becomes
26	  a disclosed :class:`Assumption` and the artifact is ``auto_pinned``. This is
27	  the load-bearing benchmark mode (a test asserts zero blocking with no input
28	  channel).
29	* **No invention.** The compiler system prompt forbids inventing constraints and
30	  requires spans for explicit terms; the deterministic
31	  :func:`~planning_gate_schema.validate_contract` then MECHANICALLY rejects any
32	  hard term that is not explicit/user-backed. A rejected contract gets ONE
33	  correction retry, then a conservative span-verified fallback
34	  (``compiler_degraded=true``) — never a guessed contract, never a crash.
35	* **Reuse.** Candidate seeding reuses the S0 ``candidate_adapter``; parsing
36	  reuses the fence-tolerant discipline of ``constraint_extractor`` /
37	  ``research_planner``; hashing reuses ``planning_gate_schema.sha256_of`` (same
38	  construction as ``research_planner.plan_sha256``).
39	
40	Nothing here is wired into the paid pipeline — that is S2+. This module is the
41	compiler + its offline harness.
42	"""

289	
290	def _extract_json_object(raw: str) -> str:
291	    """First balanced ``{...}`` object substring, fence-tolerant."""
292	    text = (raw or "").strip()
293	    if text.startswith("```"):
294	        text = text.split("\n", 1)[1] if "\n" in text else text
295	        if text.rstrip().endswith("```"):
296	            text = text.rstrip()[:-3]
297	    text = text.strip()
298	    start = text.find("{")
299	    if start == -1:
300	        return text
301	    depth = 0
302	    in_str = False
303	    esc = False
304	    for i in range(start, len(text)):
305	        ch = text[i]
306	        if in_str:
307	            if esc:
308	                esc = False
309	            elif ch == "\\":
310	                esc = True
311	            elif ch == '"':
312	                in_str = False
313	            continue
314	        if ch == '"':
315	            in_str = True
316	        elif ch == "{":
317	            depth += 1
318	        elif ch == "}":
319	            depth -= 1
320	            if depth == 0:
321	                return text[start:i + 1]
322	    return text[start:]
323	
324	
325	def _loads(raw: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
326	    obj = _extract_json_object(raw)
327	    data = json.loads(obj)
328	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
329	        raise ValueError("model output was not a JSON object")
330	    return data

371	# Deterministic conservative fallback (never a guessed contract)
372	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
373	
374	def _conservative_contract(
375	    prompt: str, candidates: list[CandidateConstraint]
376	) -> ResearchContract:
377	    """The LOSSLESS fallback core when the LLM output is unusable twice.
378	
379	    When PG_GATE is ON this is the registry-driven lossless core
380	    (:func:`_lossless_fallback_contract`): EVERY deterministic explicit constraint
381	    survives with its canonical dimension and correct stage owner (no category
382	    leakage), and an unmappable kind is kept as a first-class OPAQUE term — so
383	    "degraded" means ENRICHMENT is thin, NEVER that a stated constraint vanished.
384	
385	    When PG_GATE is OFF this is the legacy conservative fallback (byte-identical to
386	    the pre-inversion path): raw prompt as the objective, span-verified candidates
387	    kept, everything else open. It can never invent (only span-backed candidates
388	    become explicit/hard).
389	    """
390	    if gate_enabled():
391	        return _lossless_fallback_contract(prompt, candidates)
392	
393	    objective = [ContractTerm(
394	        term_id="objective.question",
395	        dimension="objective.question",
396	        value=prompt.strip(),
397	        origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if prompt.strip() else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
398	        force=FORCE_OPEN,
399	        spans=[PromptSpan(0, len(prompt), prompt)] if prompt.strip() else [],
400	        rationale="degraded fallback: raw request retained as the objective",
401	    )]
402	
403	    scope: list[ContractTerm] = []
404	    coverage: list[CoverageRequirement] = []
405	    assumptions: list[Assumption] = []
406	    n = 0
407	    for cand in candidates:
408	        span_ok = any(
409	            sp.quote == prompt[sp.start:sp.end] for sp in cand.spans
410	        ) and bool(cand.spans)
411	        # A hard candidate keeps hard force ONLY with a verbatim span; otherwise
412	        # it degrades to preference (the mechanical no-invention guarantee).
413	        force = FORCE_HARD if (cand.force == "hard" and span_ok) else FORCE_PREFER
414	        origin = ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if span_ok else ORIGIN_INFERRED
415	        n += 1
416	        tid = f"scope.cand_{n}"
417	        spans = [PromptSpan(sp.start, sp.end, sp.quote) for sp in cand.spans]
418	        term = ContractTerm(
419	            term_id=tid,
420	            dimension=cand.dimension,
421	            value=cand.value,
422	            origin=origin,
423	            force=force,
424	            spans=spans,
425	            rationale=f"degraded fallback from candidate ({cand.origin})",
426	        )
427	        if cand.dimension.startswith("content"):
428	            coverage.append(CoverageRequirement(
429	                requirement_id=tid, kind="topic", statement=term, required=False,
430	            ))
431	        else:
432	            scope.append(term)
433	        if origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS:
434	            assumptions.append(Assumption(
435	                assumption_id=f"asm_{n}",
436	                statement=f"kept {cand.dimension}={cand.value!r} as an open preference "
437	                          f"(no verbatim span to make it explicit)",
438	                affected_term_ids=[tid],
439	                origin=ORIGIN_INFERRED,
440	                consequence="ranked/routed only; never a hard gate",
441	            ))
442	
443	    return ResearchContract(
444	        objective=objective,
445	        scope=scope,
446	        coverage=coverage,
447	        assumptions=assumptions,
448	        complexity="degraded",
449	        compiler_degraded=True,
450	    )
451	
452	
453	def _lossless_fallback_contract(
454	    prompt: str, candidates: list[CandidateConstraint]
455	) -> ResearchContract:
456	    """The LOSSLESS fallback (PG_GATE ON): the deterministic-authoritative core
457	    run as the WHOLE contract when the LLM failed.
458	
459	    Every span-verified deterministic explicit source/date/exclusion constraint is
460	    authored (via the registry, canonical dimension, correct force), and every
461	    content-coverage candidate becomes a coverage requirement. Deliverable/format/
462	    length/tone candidates land in the DELIVERABLE group — NEVER scope (no category
463	    leakage). An unmappable kind is preserved as a first-class OPAQUE term. Marked
464	    ``compiler_degraded=true``: enrichment is thin, but NO explicit constraint
465	    vanished.
466	    """
467	    objective = [ContractTerm(
468	        term_id="objective.question",
469	        dimension="objective.question",
470	        value=prompt.strip(),
471	        origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if prompt.strip() else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
472	        force=FORCE_OPEN,
473	        spans=[PromptSpan(0, len(prompt), prompt)] if prompt.strip() else [],
474	        rationale="lossless fallback: raw request retained as the objective",
475	    )]
476	
477	    # authoritative source/date/exclusion terms + content-coverage terms.
478	    scope, content = _author_deterministic_terms(candidates, prompt)
479	    coverage: list[CoverageRequirement] = [
480	        CoverageRequirement(
481	            requirement_id=t.term_id, kind="topic", statement=t, required=True,
482	        )
483	        for t in content
484	    ]
485	
486	    # deliverable / rhetoric candidates: file by stage owner, NEVER in scope.
487	    deliverable: list[ContractTerm] = []
488	    n = 0
489	    for cand in candidates:
490	        if cand.dimension not in (
491	            "deliverable.format", "deliverable.length", "rhetoric.tone",
492	        ):
493	            continue
494	        span_ok = _cand_span_ok(cand, prompt)
495	        if not (cand.value or "").strip():
496	            continue
497	        n += 1
498	        deliverable.append(ContractTerm(
499	            term_id=f"deliverable.cand_{n}",
500	            dimension=cand.canonical_dimension(),
501	            value=cand.value,
502	            origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT if span_ok else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
503	            force=FORCE_PREFER,   # deliverable shape is a preference, never a gate
504	            spans=_cand_spans(cand, prompt),
505	            subject=cand.subject,
506	            attribute=cand.attribute,
507	            stage_owner=cand.stage_owner,
508	            normalization_status=cand.normalization_status or NORM_EXACT,
509	            rationale="lossless fallback: deliverable shape preserved (render-owned)",
510	        ))
511	
512	    return ResearchContract(
513	        objective=objective,
514	        scope=scope,
515	        deliverable=deliverable,
516	        coverage=coverage,
517	        complexity="degraded",
518	        compiler_degraded=True,
519	    )

522	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
523	# DETERMINISTIC-AUTHORITATIVE CORE + MONOTONIC MERGE (Phase B — the inversion)
524	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
525	#
526	# This REPLACES the deleted ``_promote_source_scope`` task-shaped whitelist. The
527	# authority is INVERTED: deterministic code (the S0 candidate adapter + the
528	# candidate→canonical registry) AUTHORS the explicit-constraint contract; the LLM
529	# is additive-only and MERGED monotonically (it may add/enrich, never delete,
530	# downgrade, or re-dimension a deterministic explicit term).
531	#
532	# No per-type branching: every source/date/exclusion/coverage family is driven by
533	# ``candidate_adapter.CANDIDATE_REGISTRY`` (the row supplies canonical dimension +
534	# subject/attribute/operator/stage_owner). An unknown kind stays a first-class
535	# OPAQUE term — preserved, never dropped.
536	#
537	# Gated behind PG_GATE (default OFF): with the flag OFF this whole core is a no-op
538	# and the compiled contract is byte-identical to the pre-inversion path (the LLM
539	# compile alone), preserving the OFF guardrail.
540	
541	# The candidate dimensions whose terms are EXPLICIT authority (a real user rule
542	# with a verbatim span) — as opposed to advisory coverage the LLM may reshape.
543	# A span-verified candidate on any of these is authored as an explicit contract
544	# term the LLM merge can neither drop nor downgrade.
545	_AUTHORITATIVE_DIMENSIONS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
546	    "source.types", "source.quality", "source.language", "source.scope_facet",
547	    "source.jurisdiction", "source.named", "date.recency", "content.exclusion",
548	})
549	
550	
551	def _cand_span_ok(cand: CandidateConstraint, prompt: str) -> bool:
552	    """A candidate carries at least one verbatim-quote-verified span."""
553	    return bool(cand.spans) and any(
554	        0 <= sp.start <= sp.end <= len(prompt) and sp.quote == prompt[sp.start:sp.end]
555	        for sp in cand.spans
556	    )
557	
558	
559	def _cand_spans(cand: CandidateConstraint, prompt: str) -> list[PromptSpan]:
560	    return [
561	        PromptSpan(sp.start, sp.end, sp.quote)
562	        for sp in cand.spans
563	        if 0 <= sp.start <= sp.end <= len(prompt) and sp.quote == prompt[sp.start:sp.end]
564	    ]
565	
566	
567	def _hard_facet_ranges(candidates: list[CandidateConstraint], prompt: str) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
568	    """Verbatim spans of hard source-KIND scope clauses. A soft source.language
569	    whose span sits INSIDE one of these (e.g. "only cites ... English-language
570	    journal articles") inherits the enclosing "only" hardness — never a
571	    fabricated hard gate (the range must itself be a span-verified hard facet)."""
572	    ranges: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
573	    for c in candidates:
574	        if c.dimension in ("source.scope_facet", "source.types") and c.force == FORCE_HARD:
575	            ranges.extend((sp.start, sp.end) for sp in _cand_spans(c, prompt))
576	    return ranges
577	
578	
579	def _term_from_candidate(
580	    cand: CandidateConstraint, prompt: str, idx: int,
581	    *, hard_ranges: list[tuple[int, int]],
582	) -> Optional[ContractTerm]:
583	    """Author ONE explicit contract term from a span-verified deterministic
584	    candidate, via the registry (generic — no per-type branch). Returns None when
585	    the candidate has no verbatim span (never a fabricated explicit/hard term).
586	
587	    Force: the candidate's own observed force, EXCEPT a soft source.language whose
588	    span is enclosed by a hard source-kind clause, which inherits hard. Origin is
589	    always ``explicit`` (a span-verified user rule). ``normalization_status``
590	    carries through: an opaque candidate becomes an opaque (but preserved) term.
591	    """
592	    spans = _cand_spans(cand, prompt)
593	    if not spans:
594	        return None
595	    row = None
596	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.candidate_adapter import _registry_row  # noqa: PLC0415
597	    row = _registry_row(cand.dimension)
598	    canonical_dim = row.canonical if row else cand.dimension
599	    value = (cand.value or "").strip()
600	    if not value:
601	        return None
602	
603	    force = FORCE_HARD if cand.force == FORCE_HARD else FORCE_PREFER
604	    # language-inside-a-hard-only-clause inheritance (the sole cross-term rule; it
605	    # never invents — it only propagates an already-hard enclosing span).
606	    if canonical_dim == SCOPE_SOURCE_LANGUAGES and force != FORCE_HARD:
607	        for sp in spans:
608	            if any(lo <= sp.start and sp.end <= hi for lo, hi in hard_ranges):
609	                force = FORCE_HARD
610	                break
611	
612	    norm = cand.normalization_status or NORM_EXACT
613	    # An OPAQUE hard term is preserved but cannot be claimed enforceable — it is
614	    # surfaced (the artifact's enforcement state becomes blocked_unsupported).
615	    operator = cand.operator or ""
616	    value_set = [value] if operator in ("IN", "NOT_IN") else []
617	    return ContractTerm(
618	        term_id=candidate_term_id(cand),
619	        dimension=canonical_dim,
620	        value=value,
621	        origin=ORIGIN_EXPLICIT,
622	        force=force,
623	        spans=spans,
624	        subject=cand.subject or (row.subject if row else "source"),
625	        attribute=cand.attribute or (row.attribute if row else ""),
626	        operator=operator,
627	        value_set=value_set,
628	        stage_owner=cand.stage_owner or (row.stage_owner if row else ""),
629	        normalization_status=norm,
630	        enforcement_stages=["retrieval"],
631	        rationale="deterministic-authoritative: span-verified explicit constraint "
632	                  "(the LLM merge may enrich but never drop/downgrade it)",
633	    )
634	
635	
636	def _author_deterministic_terms(
637	    candidates: list[CandidateConstraint], prompt: str,
638	) -> tuple[list[ContractTerm], list[ContractTerm]]:
639	    """Author the deterministic explicit contract terms from candidates.
640	
641	    Returns ``(scope_terms, coverage_content_terms)`` — scope/source/date/
642	    exclusion terms vs content-coverage terms — so the caller files each in the
643	    right group with NO category leakage. De-duplicates by canonical term_id
644	    (hard wins over prefer; spans unioned).
645	    """
646	    hard_ranges = _hard_facet_ranges(candidates, prompt)
647	    by_id: dict[str, ContractTerm] = {}
648	    for i, cand in enumerate(candidates):
649	        if cand.dimension not in _AUTHORITATIVE_DIMENSIONS:
650	            continue
651	        term = _term_from_candidate(cand, prompt, i, hard_ranges=hard_ranges)
652	        if term is None:
653	            continue
654	        prev = by_id.get(term.term_id)
655	        if prev is None:
656	            by_id[term.term_id] = term
657	        else:
658	            # merge duplicates: hard wins; union spans.
659	            if term.force == FORCE_HARD:
660	                prev.force = FORCE_HARD
661	            seen = {(s.start, s.end) for s in prev.spans}
662	            prev.spans.extend(s for s in term.spans if (s.start, s.end) not in seen)
663	
664	    scope: list[ContractTerm] = []
665	    content: list[ContractTerm] = []
666	    for term in by_id.values():
667	        if term.dimension.startswith("content"):
668	            content.append(term)
669	        else:
670	            scope.append(term)
671	    return scope, content
672	
673	
674	def _merge_deterministic_authority(
675	    contract: ResearchContract,
676	    candidates: list[CandidateConstraint],
677	    prompt: str,
678	) -> ResearchContract:
679	    """MONOTONIC MERGE: reinstate every deterministic explicit term as authority.
680	
681	    The LLM contract is treated as ADDITIVE. For each deterministic explicit term:
682	      * if the LLM already carries a term on the SAME canonical (dimension,value),
683	        that term is UPGRADED to explicit + the deterministic force (hard wins),
684	        and given the deterministic spans if it lacked them — the LLM enriched it,
685	        the deterministic core owns its force/origin;
686	      * otherwise the deterministic term is APPENDED to the correct group.
687	    The LLM may have ADDED its own terms; those are left untouched. It can never
688	    delete/downgrade/re-dimension a deterministic term — that is the inversion.
689	    Mutates + returns the contract. No-op when PG_GATE is OFF (OFF byte-identical).
690	    """
691	    if not gate_enabled():
692	        return contract
693	
694	    prompt = prompt or ""
695	    det_scope, det_content = _author_deterministic_terms(candidates, prompt)
696	
697	    def _key(dim: str, value: Any) -> tuple[str, str]:
698	        return (dim, str(value or "").strip().casefold())
699	
700	    # index existing scope terms for overlap upgrade.
701	    existing: dict[tuple[str, str], ContractTerm] = {}
702	    for t in contract.scope:
703	        existing[_key(t.dimension, t.value)] = t
704	
705	    for det in det_scope:
706	        key = _key(det.dimension, det.value)
707	        llm_term = existing.get(key)
708	        if llm_term is not None:
709	            # LLM enriched an overlapping term: deterministic owns force/origin/span.
710	            llm_term.origin = ORIGIN_EXPLICIT
711	            if det.force == FORCE_HARD:
712	                llm_term.force = FORCE_HARD
713	            elif llm_term.force != FORCE_HARD:
714	                llm_term.force = det.force
715	            if not llm_term.spans:
716	                llm_term.spans = list(det.spans)
717	            # carry the generic-IR annotations the LLM couldn't be trusted to set.
718	            llm_term.subject = llm_term.subject or det.subject
719	            llm_term.attribute = llm_term.attribute or det.attribute
720	            llm_term.operator = llm_term.operator or det.operator
721	            llm_term.value_set = llm_term.value_set or det.value_set
722	            llm_term.stage_owner = llm_term.stage_owner or det.stage_owner
723	            llm_term.normalization_status = det.normalization_status
724	            llm_term.rationale = (
725	                (llm_term.rationale + " | ") if llm_term.rationale else ""
726	            ) + "deterministic authority upheld (monotonic merge)"
727	        else:
728	            contract.scope.append(det)
729	            existing[key] = det
730	
731	    # content-coverage terms: reinstate as required coverage requirements if the
732	    # LLM dropped them (exclusions in particular must never be lost).
733	    existing_cov_vals = {
734	        str(cr.statement.value or "").strip().casefold() for cr in contract.coverage
735	    }
736	    for det in det_content:
737	        v = str(det.value or "").strip().casefold()
738	        if v and v not in existing_cov_vals:
739	            contract.coverage.append(CoverageRequirement(
740	                requirement_id=det.term_id,
741	                kind="topic",
742	                statement=det,
743	                required=True,
744	            ))
745	            existing_cov_vals.add(v)
746	
747	    return contract
748	
749	
750	def _deterministic_candidate_ids(
751	    candidates: list[CandidateConstraint], prompt: str,
752	) -> set[str]:
753	    """The stable term_ids of every span-verified deterministic explicit
754	    candidate — the set ``validate_monotonicity`` requires survived the merge."""
755	    ids: set[str] = set()
756	    for cand in candidates:
757	        if cand.dimension in _AUTHORITATIVE_DIMENSIONS and _cand_span_ok(cand, prompt):
758	            if (cand.value or "").strip():
759	                ids.add(candidate_term_id(cand))
760	    return ids
761	
762	
763	def _enforcement_state(contract: ResearchContract) -> str:
764	    """Derive the honest enforcement state (spec deliverable 6). A hard OPAQUE
765	    term (or a hard term with no executable path) → blocked_unsupported; a
766	    degraded compile → degraded_lossless; else pinned_executable."""
767	    for term in contract.hard_terms():
768	        if term.is_opaque():
769	            return GATE_ENFORCEMENT_BLOCKED
770	    # Phase D seam: a hard term with no registered capability is blocked. The stub
771	    # returns [] today (capability registry lands in Phase D), so this is inert.
772	    if validate_capabilities(contract):
773	        return GATE_ENFORCEMENT_BLOCKED
774	    if contract.compiler_degraded:
775	        return GATE_ENFORCEMENT_DEGRADED
776	    return GATE_ENFORCEMENT_PINNED
777

779	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
780	# Autonomous disclosure sweep (least-restrictive + every inferred term recorded)
781	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
782	
783	def _autonomous_disclose(contract: ResearchContract) -> ResearchContract:
784	    """Ensure the autonomous invariant: every inferred/policy_default term with a
785	    value has an :class:`Assumption`, and NO hard term survives without an
786	    authoritative origin (defense in depth against a slipped-through hard).
787	
788	    This mutates the contract in place and returns it. It NEVER blocks and NEVER
789	    asks — it is the pure ``contract→contract`` step that lets autonomous mode
790	    proceed with disclosed assumptions instead of questions.
791	    """
792	    disclosed = set()
793	    for a in contract.assumptions:
794	        disclosed.update(a.affected_term_ids)
795	
796	    next_idx = len(contract.assumptions)
797	    for term in contract.all_terms():
798	        # Belt: downgrade any hard term whose origin cannot back a hard force.
799	        if term.is_hard() and term.origin not in HARD_ELIGIBLE_ORIGINS:
800	            term.force = FORCE_PREFER
801	            term.rationale = (
802	                (term.rationale + " | ") if term.rationale else ""
803	            ) + "autonomous: downgraded hard→preference (non-explicit origin)"
804	
805	        if (
806	            term.origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS
807	            and term.value not in (None, "", [], {})
808	            and term.term_id
809	            and term.term_id not in disclosed
810	        ):
811	            next_idx += 1
812	            contract.assumptions.append(Assumption(
813	                assumption_id=f"auto_asm_{next_idx}",
814	                statement=f"assumed {term.dimension}={term.value!r} "
815	                          f"(least-restrictive default; unspecified in the request)",
816	                affected_term_ids=[term.term_id],
817	                origin=term.origin if term.origin in DISCLOSURE_ORIGINS else ORIGIN_INFERRED,
818	                consequence="preference/routing only; never a hard gate",
819	                reversible=True,
820	            ))
821	            disclosed.add(term.term_id)
822	
823	    return contract
824	
825	
826	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
827	# Ask-vs-assume (interactive only; ≤3 material questions)
828	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
829	
830	def _material_questions(contract: ResearchContract, limit: int = 3) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
831	    """Generate ≤``limit`` material questions from the contract's ambiguities.
832	
833	    Eligible only if the ambiguity is marked material AND cannot proceed open.
834	    Autonomous mode NEVER calls this. Coupled ambiguities are de-duplicated by
835	    their affected term set.
836	    """
837	    seen_keys: set[tuple[str, ...]] = set()
838	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
839	    for amb in contract.ambiguities:
840	        if not amb.material or amb.can_proceed_open:
841	            continue
842	        key = tuple(sorted(amb.affected_term_ids))
843	        if key in seen_keys:
844	            continue
845	        seen_keys.add(key)
846	        out.append({
847	            "question_id": f"q_{len(out) + 1}",
848	            "prompt": amb.text,
849	            "choices": list(amb.plausible_interpretations) + ["keep broad / use best judgment"],
850	            "affected_term_ids": list(amb.affected_term_ids),
851	            "why_it_matters": ", ".join(amb.decision_impact) or "materially changes the plan",
852	        })
853	        if len(out) >= limit:
854	            break
855	    return out
856

859	# LLM call plumbing
860	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
861	
862	async def _call(
863	    client: Any, system: str, user: str, max_tokens: int
864	) -> str:
865	    # I-gate-089/FX-01: bound the reasoning-first pool so a fixed content slice
866	    # (max_tokens - pool) always survives — otherwise glm-5.2's effort=high
867	    # reasoning prelude eats the whole budget and content truncates at
868	    # finish_reason='length'. Cap the pool at half the budget so content never
869	    # loses; glm-5.2's branch-1 honors reasoning.max_tokens. Passed positionally-
870	    # safe as a kwarg so the OFF/stub paths (which ignore **kwargs) stay identical.
871	    _reasoning_pool = min(_PLANNING_GATE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS, max(max_tokens // 2, 1))
872	    response = await client.generate(
873	        prompt=user,
874	        system=system,
875	        max_tokens=max_tokens,
876	        temperature=0.0,
877	        reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_pool,
878	    )
879	    return getattr(response, "content", None) or ""
880	
881	
882	def _errors_block(errors: list[ValidationError]) -> str:
883	    return json.dumps([e.to_dict() for e in errors], ensure_ascii=False)
884	
885	
886	# Validator error codes that are REPAIRABLE by the autonomous disclosure sweep
887	# (or are advisory), so they must NOT force the conservative fallback / discard a
888	# good contract. Everything else (the no-invention + span invariants: bad enums,
889	# hard_not_explicit, explicit_without_span, span_quote_mismatch, duplicate ids)
890	# is FATAL and drives the retry → fallback path.
891	_REPAIRABLE_CODES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
892	    "inferred_not_disclosed",
893	    "mandatory_lane_count_mismatch",
894	})
895	
896	
897	# In AUTONOMOUS mode an inferred-hard that survives the retry is not fatal: the
898	# disclosure sweep downgrades it hard→preference (never a fabricated hard gate),
899	# so it must not nuke an otherwise-good contract to the raw-prompt fallback. In
900	# interactive mode it stays fatal (a human can fix it). The span-integrity checks
901	# stay fatal in BOTH modes — a bad span can never be silently kept.
902	_AUTONOMOUS_REPAIRABLE_CODES: frozenset[str] = _REPAIRABLE_CODES | frozenset({
903	    "hard_not_explicit",
904	})
905	
906	
907	def _fatal_errors(
908	    errors: list[ValidationError], *, mode: str = "interactive"
909	) -> list[ValidationError]:
910	    repairable = (
911	        _AUTONOMOUS_REPAIRABLE_CODES if mode == "autonomous" else _REPAIRABLE_CODES
912	    )
913	    return [e for e in errors if e.code not in repairable]

916	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
917	# The gate entry point
918	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
919	
920	async def run_research_planning_gate(
921	    prompt: str,
922	    *,
923	    mode: str,
924	    client: Any = None,
925	    rule_reader: Any = None,
926	    ontology: Any = None,
927	    run_id: Optional[str] = None,
928	    model: Optional[str] = None,
929	) -> GateResult:
930	    """Compile ``prompt`` into a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`.
931	
932	    Parameters
933	    ----------
934	    prompt:
935	        The raw task prompt.
936	    mode:
937	        ``"autonomous"`` (benchmark — never blocks) or ``"interactive"`` (may
938	        return ``needs_input`` with ≤3 material questions). The caller passes it
939	        EXPLICITLY; the gate never infers mode from a terminal/timeout.
940	    client:
941	        An LLM client with an async ``generate(prompt, system, max_tokens,
942	        temperature)`` returning an object with ``.content``. Tests inject a
943	        stub. When ``None``, a real :class:`OpenRouterClient` is built ONLY if
944	        ``PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1``; otherwise this raises (the OFF path never
945	        fires an LLM).
946	    rule_reader / ontology:
947	        Forwarded to the S0 ``reconcile_candidates`` candidate seeding.
948	
949	    Returns
950	    -------
951	    GateResult
952	        Autonomous: ``state`` is ``auto_pinned`` (or ``unsatisfiable`` on a fatal
953	        explicit contradiction), ``needs_input`` ALWAYS False. Interactive:
954	        ``needs_input`` True iff there are material questions, else ``approved``.
955	    """
956	    mode = (mode or "").strip().lower()
957	    if mode not in ("interactive", "autonomous"):
958	        raise ValueError(
959	            f"mode must be 'interactive' or 'autonomous', got {mode!r} "
960	            f"(the caller passes it explicitly; the gate never infers it)"
961	        )
962	
963	    if client is None:
964	        if not _live_enabled():
965	            raise RuntimeError(
966	                "research planning gate: live LLM disabled. Set "
967	                "PG_PLANNING_GATE_LIVE=1 for a real call, or pass client= in tests."
968	            )
969	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
970	            OpenRouterClient,
971	        )
972	        client = OpenRouterClient(model=model or _resolve_model())
973	
974	    run_id = run_id or f"gate_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
975	
976	    # --- 1. deterministic candidate seeding (S0 adapter) ---
977	    try:
978	        candidates = reconcile_candidates(
979	            prompt, rule_reader=rule_reader, ontology=ontology
980	        )
981	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — candidates are advisory; fail-open to none
982	        candidates = []
983	
984	    # --- 1b. LOSSLESS CLAUSE LEDGER (Phase B — the generality gap) ---
985	    # Segment the prompt into stable deontic-classified clauses and run the
986	    # deterministic ledger parsers (quality / exclusion / coordination / date-
987	    # hardness-inheritance) that catch the constraints the champion intake
988	    # extractor structurally misses. The augmenting candidates are folded into
989	    # the candidate list so the existing deterministic-authoritative merge authors
990	    # them. Fully behind PG_GATE: OFF → no clauses, no ledger candidates, and the
991	    # candidate list + contract are byte-identical to the pre-ledger path.
992	    clauses: list[Any] = []
993	    if gate_enabled():
994	        try:
995	            clauses = segment_clauses(prompt)
996	            candidates = candidates + ledger_candidates(
997	                prompt, clauses, ontology=ontology
998	            )
999	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — ledger is additive; fail-open to none
1000	            clauses = []
1001	
1002	    # --- 2. contract compile (+ one correction retry, then fallback) ---
1003	    contract, contract_errors, degraded = await _compile_contract(
1004	        client, prompt, candidates, mode=mode
1005	    )
1006	
1007	    # --- 2b. DETERMINISTIC-AUTHORITATIVE MONOTONIC MERGE (the inversion) ---
1008	    # The LLM contract is ADDITIVE ONLY. Deterministic code (candidate adapter +
1009	    # registry) authored the explicit constraints; here they are reinstated as
1010	    # authority — the LLM can neither drop, downgrade, nor re-dimension them. This
1011	    # subsumes the deleted task-shaped ``_promote_source_scope``. No-op
1012	    # (byte-identical to the pure-LLM compile) when PG_GATE is OFF.
1013	    contract = _merge_deterministic_authority(contract, candidates, prompt)
1014	    # --- 2c. LOSSLESS OPAQUE PRESERVATION (the key invariant) ---
1015	    # Every deontic-marked constraint clause that no candidate span covered becomes
1016	    # a first-class OPAQUE ContractTerm (preserved verbatim, force from its deontic
1017	    # cue, origin=explicit, normalization_status=opaque → blocked_unsupported). This
1018	    # is what makes the gate lossless REGARDLESS of whether a deterministic
1019	    # extractor exists — the recon's press-release / blog / bare-quality misses can
1020	    # no longer vanish. No-op when PG_GATE is OFF (clauses is empty).
1021	    if gate_enabled() and clauses:
1022	        for opaque_term in opaque_terms_for_uncovered(prompt, clauses, contract):
1023	            contract.scope.append(opaque_term)
1024	    contract_errors = validate_contract(contract, prompt)
1025	    # MONOTONICITY + COMPLETENESS invariants (the lossless gate). Computed into a
1026	    # separate list so the autonomous re-validation below (which recomputes the
1027	    # base ``validate_contract`` after the disclosure sweep) cannot silently drop
1028	    # them — the load-bearing autonomous mode must still surface a dropped
1029	    # candidate / an undispositioned deontic constraint clause.
1030	    #   * MONOTONICITY: every span-verified deterministic explicit candidate
1031	    #     survived the merge (belt — the merge already reinstates them).
1032	    #   * COMPLETENESS: every deontic-marked constraint clause has a corresponding
1033	    #     term (normalized OR opaque). Keys off the PROMPT (the ledger), not the
1034	    #     extractor output — the check candidate-only monotonicity structurally
1035	    #     cannot make. The opaque-preservation sweep above backfills uncovered
1036	    #     clauses, so this is the residual belt.
1037	    ledger_invariant_errors: list[ValidationError] = []
1038	    if gate_enabled():
1039	        ledger_invariant_errors = validate_monotonicity(
1040	            contract, _deterministic_candidate_ids(candidates, prompt)
1041	        )
1042	        if clauses:
1043	            ledger_invariant_errors = ledger_invariant_errors + validate_completeness(
1044	                clauses, contract
1045	            )
1046	    contract_errors = contract_errors + ledger_invariant_errors
1047	
1048	    # --- 3. mode split (the ONLY node the two modes differ at) ---
1049	    if mode == "autonomous":
1050	        contract = _autonomous_disclose(contract)
1051	        # re-validate after disclosure (should be clean; record if not). Re-append
1052	        # the ledger invariants so the autonomous path cannot lose them.
1053	        contract_errors = validate_contract(contract, prompt) + ledger_invariant_errors
1054	        fatal = any(c.fatal for c in contract.conflicts)
1055	        state = "unsatisfiable" if fatal else "auto_pinned"
1056	        questions: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
1057	        needs_input = False
1058	    else:
1059	        questions = _material_questions(contract)
1060	        needs_input = bool(questions)
1061	        state = "needs_input" if needs_input else "approved"
1062

1115	async def _compile_contract(
1116	    client: Any,
1117	    prompt: str,
1118	    candidates: list[CandidateConstraint],
1119	    *,
1120	    mode: str = "interactive",
1121	) -> tuple[ResearchContract, list[ValidationError], bool]:
1122	    """Contract compile with one bounded correction retry, then conservative
1123	    fallback. Returns ``(contract, remaining_errors, degraded)``.
1124	
1125	    The retry fires on ANY validation error (push the compiler to fix even
1126	    repairable ones). The final keep-vs-fallback decision uses the MODE-AWARE
1127	    fatal set: in autonomous mode an inferred-hard the compiler wouldn't fix is
1128	    downgraded by the disclosure sweep rather than nuking the whole contract to
1129	    the raw-prompt fallback; span-integrity violations stay fatal in both modes.
1130	    """
1131	    user = _contract_user_prompt(prompt, candidates, mode="compile")
1132	    try:
1133	        raw = await _call(client, _CONTRACT_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user, _CONTRACT_MAX_TOKENS)
1134	        data = _loads(raw)
1135	        contract = contract_from_dict(data.get("contract", data))
1136	        # The LLM copies the verbatim quote correctly but drifts on character
1137	        # offsets; re-derive each span from its quote (no-invention: a quote not
1138	        # present in the prompt is left to fail validation). Mirrors the S0
1139	        # candidate adapter's _locate_span discipline.
1140	        reanchor_contract_spans(contract, prompt)
1141	        errors = validate_contract(contract, prompt)
1142	        if not _fatal_errors(errors, mode=mode):
1143	            # No mode-fatal violation. Any remaining errors are repairable (an
1144	            # undisclosed inferred term the autonomous sweep backfills, or an
1145	            # inferred-hard the sweep downgrades), so keep the good contract.
1146	            return contract, errors, False
1147	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
1148	        logger.warning("contract compile failed (attempt 1): %s", exc)
1149	        errors = [ValidationError("compile_exception", str(exc))]
1150	        contract = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
1151	
1152	    # --- one correction retry with machine-readable errors ---
1153	    retry_user = (
1154	        user
1155	        + "\n\nYour previous output was rejected by the deterministic validator "
1156	          "with these errors. Fix EVERY one — especially any hard term that is "
1157	          "not origin=explicit, and any explicit term whose span does not quote "
1158	          "the request exactly. Return corrected JSON only.\n"
1159	        + _errors_block(errors)
1160	    )
1161	    try:
1162	        raw2 = await _call(client, _CONTRACT_SYSTEM_PROMPT, retry_user, _CONTRACT_MAX_TOKENS)
1163	        data2 = _loads(raw2)
1164	        contract2 = contract_from_dict(data2.get("contract", data2))
1165	        reanchor_contract_spans(contract2, prompt)
1166	        errors2 = validate_contract(contract2, prompt)
1167	        if not _fatal_errors(errors2, mode=mode):
1168	            return contract2, errors2, False
1169	        # retry still has a mode-fatal violation -> conservative fallback
1170	        logger.warning(
1171	            "contract compile still invalid after retry: %d fatal errors",
1172	            len(_fatal_errors(errors2, mode=mode)),
1173	        )
1174	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
1175	        logger.warning("contract compile failed (attempt 2): %s", exc)
1176	
1177	    fallback = _conservative_contract(prompt, candidates)
1178	    return fallback, validate_contract(fallback, prompt), True
1179	
1180	
1181	async def _compile_plan(
1182	    client: Any, contract: ResearchContract
1183	) -> tuple[ResearchExecutionPlan, list[ValidationError]]:
1184	    """Plan compile with one bounded correction retry, then a deterministic
1185	    one-thread-per-required-clause fallback (never drop a clause)."""
1186	    user = _plan_user_prompt(contract)
1187	    try:
1188	        raw = await _call(client, _PLAN_SYSTEM_PROMPT, user, _PLAN_MAX_TOKENS)
1189	        data = _loads(raw)
1190	        plan = plan_from_dict(data.get("plan", data))
1191	        errors = validate_plan(plan, contract)
1192	        if not errors:
1193	            return plan, []
1194	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
1195	        logger.warning("plan compile failed (attempt 1): %s", exc)
1196	        errors = [ValidationError("compile_exception", str(exc))]
1197	        plan = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
1198	
1199	    retry_user = (
1200	        user
1201	        + "\n\nYour previous plan was rejected by the validator with these "
1202	          "errors. Fix EVERY one — give every required coverage requirement a "
1203	          "mandatory query intent via a thread, and bind every hard contract "
1204	          "term in coverage_matrix. Return corrected JSON only.\n"
1205	        + _errors_block(errors)
1206	    )
1207	    try:
1208	        raw2 = await _call(client, _PLAN_SYSTEM_PROMPT, retry_user, _PLAN_MAX_TOKENS)
1209	        data2 = _loads(raw2)
1210	        plan2 = plan_from_dict(data2.get("plan", data2))
1211	        errors2 = validate_plan(plan2, contract)
1212	        if not errors2:
1213	            return plan2, []
1214	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
1215	        logger.warning("plan compile failed (attempt 2): %s", exc)
1216	
1217	    return _fallback_plan(contract), []
1218	
1219	
1220	def _fallback_plan(contract: ResearchContract) -> ResearchExecutionPlan:
1221	    """Deterministic one-thread-per-required-clause plan — never drops a clause."""
1222	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1223	        BudgetEnvelope,
1224	        CoverageBinding,
1225	        QueryIntent,
1226	        ResearchThread,
1227	    )
1228	
1229	    threads: list[ResearchThread] = []
1230	    intents: list[QueryIntent] = []
1231	    matrix: list[CoverageBinding] = []
1232	
1233	    reqs = [c for c in contract.coverage if c.required] or contract.coverage
1234	    if not reqs:
1235	        # a narrow prompt with no explicit coverage: one discovery thread on the
1236	        # objective (never fabricate facets).
1237	        obj_val = ""
1238	        for t in contract.objective:
1239	            if t.value:
1240	                obj_val = str(t.value)
1241	                break
1242	        threads.append(ResearchThread(
1243	            thread_id="t1", question=obj_val, purpose="objective discovery",
1244	            mandatory=True,
1245	        ))
1246	        intents.append(QueryIntent(
1247	            intent_id="qi1", thread_id="t1", purpose="discovery",
1248	            concepts=[obj_val] if obj_val else [], mandatory=True,
1249	        ))
1250	    else:
1251	        for i, cr in enumerate(reqs, 1):
1252	            tid = f"t{i}"
1253	            qid = f"qi{i}"
1254	            stmt = str(cr.statement.value or cr.requirement_id or f"requirement {i}")
1255	            threads.append(ResearchThread(
1256	                thread_id=tid, question=stmt, purpose="cover requirement",
1257	                coverage_requirement_ids=[cr.requirement_id] if cr.requirement_id else [],
1258	                mandatory=True,
1259	            ))
1260	            intents.append(QueryIntent(
1261	                intent_id=qid, thread_id=tid, purpose="discovery",
1262	                concepts=[stmt], mandatory=True,
1263	            ))
1264	
1265	    for term in contract.hard_terms():
1266	        if term.term_id:
1267	            matrix.append(CoverageBinding(
1268	                contract_term_id=term.term_id,
1269	                owning_stages=["retrieval"],
1270	                audit_method="deterministic",
1271	            ))
1272	
1273	    return ResearchExecutionPlan(
1274	        threads=threads,
1275	        query_intents=intents,
1276	        coverage_matrix=matrix,
1277	        budget=BudgetEnvelope(
1278	            mandatory_lane_count=len(intents),
1279	            overflow_policy="expand",
1280	        ),
1281	        stop_conditions=["every mandatory thread has usable evidence of the required kind"],
1282	    )

1	"""Retrieval projection (S2) — the no-starvation core, compile-only + offline.
2	
3	This is the typed *retrieval view* of a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`
4	(contract + plan). It is the S2 lever the consolidated design (§5, §4 retrieval
5	row) calls out: the gate's scope must reach FS-Researcher **query generation**
6	and backend **routing** BEFORE the first fetch — never as a post-fetch filter of
7	a frozen corpus (the banned 997->131 anti-pattern).
8	
9	A :class:`RetrievalProjection` COMPILES the contract+plan into exactly the three
10	levers ``live_retriever.run_live_retrieval`` already exposes but the driver never
11	populates from the gate:
12	
13	  * ``research_frame``  — a :class:`research_planner.ResearchFrame` whose
14	    ``evidence_needs`` are ROUTED from the contract's ``source_types`` (journal /
15	    academic / scholarly -> ``primary_literature`` = *GO FIND journals*, routing
16	    the S2 / OpenAlex scholarly adapters), plus entities / comparators /
17	    jurisdictions for the field-agnostic need-type registry;
18	  * ``amplified_queries`` — query TEXT built from the plan's MANDATORY query
19	    intents: concepts + required-terms, plus native-language sub-queries for a
20	    hard ``source_languages`` term, plus per-entity sub-queries, plus the hard
21	    scope terms folded into the query text so a "journal-only" rule shapes
22	    discovery from the first query;
23	  * ``protocol`` — the frame's anchor protocol (``to_anchor_protocol``), so the
24	    amplified sub-queries validate against the frame's tokens.
25	
26	Guardrail posture
27	-----------------
28	* **Compile-only. No network, no LLM, no I/O.** This module builds plain data
29	  (lists of strings, a dataclass) from an already-pinned artifact. It NEVER
30	  fetches. The only cross-module import is the champion ``ResearchFrame`` (a pure
31	  dataclass) which it constructs; that construction runs the frame's own
32	  ``validate_evidence_needs`` so a routed need is always a valid enum member.
33	* **ADD / ROUTE, never filter.** Every method returns queries or routing hints
34	  that ENLARGE or STEER discovery. There is no drop path here. The mechanized
35	  997-guard (:func:`candidate_query_count`) counts the candidate query/evidence
36	  lanes so a caller can assert the gate path never REDUCES the lane count vs the
37	  no-gate path — at planning level, before any fetch.
38	* **Hard vs soft.** A HARD scope term (``force==hard``, which the schema
39	  guarantees is explicit/user-backed) is folded into query TEXT and routed to a
40	  backend need. A SOFT term (preference/open) contributes ranking/entity breadth
41	  but is NEVER allowed to gate. This module cannot introduce a hard filter — it
42	  only emits query strings + need tokens.
43	* **Fail-open.** Every builder tolerates a degraded/empty contract: an empty
44	  contract yields an empty projection (no needs, no amplified queries), so the
45	  caller reads the champion default (``research_frame=None`` path) and the run is
46	  byte-identical. Constructing the projection NEVER raises for a shape problem.
47	
48	Nothing here is wired ON by default. ``fs_researcher_query_gen`` /
49	``live_retriever`` / ``run_one_query`` accept a ``retrieval_plan=None`` kwarg
50	(default None => champion behaviour); the projection is threaded only when the
51	``PG_GATE`` flag is set and an artifact exists. See :mod:`gate_flags`.
52	"""

114	def _norm(s: Any) -> str:

118	def source_type_to_need(source_type: Any) -> Optional[str]:

137	    """Load the scope facet ontology, or ``{}`` on any fault (fail-open: the bridge
138	    then emits opaque facet ids and the enforcer masks nothing)."""

148	def _resolve_facet_id(value: Any, ontology: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, str]:
149	    """Map a contract source-kind VALUE (e.g. ``"journal article"``) to an ontology
150	    ``(facet_id, dimension)`` via the SAME synonym table the deterministic scope
151	    extractor uses (LAW VI — no new vocabulary). A value matching no facet synonym
152	    is returned as an OPAQUE id (``opaque:<value>``, dimension ``source_type``) so it
153	    is NEVER dropped — it simply classifies no source (fail-open), staying disclosed.
154	    """
155	    v = _norm(value)
156	    if not v:
157	        return "", "source_type"
158	    best: tuple[str, str, int] = ("", "source_type", -1)
159	    for facet in (ontology.get("facets") or []):
160	        if not isinstance(facet, dict):
161	            continue
162	        fid = str(facet.get("id") or "")
163	        dim = str(facet.get("dimension") or "source_type")
164	        if not fid:
165	            continue
166	        for syn in (facet.get("synonyms") or []):
167	            synl = str(syn).strip().lower()
168	            if not synl:
169	                continue
170	            # longest matching synonym wins (most specific) — substring both ways to
171	            # tolerate plurals ("journal articles" == "journal article").
172	            if synl in v or v in synl:
173	                if len(synl) > best[2]:
174	                    best = (fid, dim, len(synl))
175	    if best[0]:
176	        return best[0], best[1]
177	    return f"opaque:{v}", "source_type"
178

185	@dataclass
186	class RetrievalPolicy:
187	    """A typed, hash-stamped policy compiled from the pinned contract + plan.
188	
189	    This is the enforcement-facing companion to :class:`RetrievalProjection`
190	    (which owns query TEXT + routing). ``RetrievalPolicy`` is the DECLARATIVE
191	    predicate set a caller executes as backend filters, prefetch ranking, and a
192	    post-fetch citable-eligibility verdict — ALWAYS upstream of the frozen
193	    verifier (it changes which rows are ELIGIBLE to cite, never HOW a claim is
194	    verified). It is pure data: no network, no LLM, no I/O.
195	
196	    Fields:
197	      * ``allowed_source_kinds`` — hard ``scope.source_types`` values. A candidate
198	        whose kind matches NONE (when non-empty) is out of the citable menu.
199	      * ``excluded_source_kinds`` — NEGATIVE predicate (``scope.prohibited`` etc.).
200	        A candidate whose kind matches ANY is masked. NEVER positive query text.
201	      * ``date_from`` / ``date_to`` — inclusive publication-date interval (ISO
202	        ``YYYY-MM-DD`` or ``YYYY``), routed server-side to OpenAlex
203	        ``from/to_publication_date`` where a backend supports it.
204	      * ``languages`` — hard ``scope.source_languages`` codes/names.
205	      * ``named_inclusions`` / ``named_exclusions`` — specific named sources/domains
206	        to pin in / mask out.
207	      * ``quality_profile`` — a REF to a domain-neutral quality profile (e.g.
208	        "high") selected over already-fetched metadata; never a query word.
209	      * ``predicate_force`` — per-predicate ``{predicate_key: "hard"|"soft"}`` so a
210	        caller knows which are gating vs ranking-only.
211	      * ``contract_hash`` — the pinned ``contract_sha256`` this policy was compiled
212	        from, so retrieval/audit can assert hash identity with the compiler.
213	    """
214	
215	    allowed_source_kinds: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
216	    excluded_source_kinds: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
217	    date_from: Optional[str] = None
218	    date_to: Optional[str] = None
219	    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
220	    named_inclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
221	    named_exclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
222	    quality_profile: Optional[str] = None
223	    predicate_force: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
224	    contract_hash: str = ""
225	
226	    def is_empty(self) -> bool:
227	        """True when the policy carries no executable predicate (the permissive /
228	        empty-contract case) — a caller then applies NO filter (byte-identical)."""
229	        return not (
230	            self.allowed_source_kinds
231	            or self.excluded_source_kinds
232	            or self.date_from
233	            or self.date_to
234	            or self.languages
235	            or self.named_inclusions
236	            or self.named_exclusions
237	            or self.quality_profile
238	        )
239	
240	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
241	        return {
242	            "allowed_source_kinds": list(self.allowed_source_kinds),
243	            "excluded_source_kinds": list(self.excluded_source_kinds),
244	            "date_from": self.date_from,
245	            "date_to": self.date_to,
246	            "languages": list(self.languages),
247	            "named_inclusions": list(self.named_inclusions),
248	            "named_exclusions": list(self.named_exclusions),
249	            "quality_profile": self.quality_profile,
250	            "predicate_force": dict(self.predicate_force),
251	            "contract_hash": self.contract_hash,
252	        }
